message:
fix(glamour): use ANSI-aware width for table column calculations

Updates table column width calculations to use visible_width() instead
of unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(). This ensures that ANSI escape
sequences (colors, styles) are not counted as visible characters when
determining column widths.

Affected functions:
- calculate_column_widths(): Now calls measure_width() for both header
  and body cells
- measure_width(): Now delegates to crate::visible_width()
- pad_content(): Uses measure_width() for accurate padding
- truncate_content(): Uses measure_width() for width calculation
  (note: TODO added for full ANSI-aware truncation)

This complements the earlier word_wrap fix to provide consistent
ANSI-aware string measurement across the glamour crate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
